source: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
title: Communicable disease threats report, 2-8 May 2026, Week 19
summary: This issue of the ECDC Communicable Disease Threats Report (CDTR) covers the period 2-8 May 2026, and includes updates on hantavirus, mpox, Middle East respiratory syndrome coronavirus (MERS-CoV), Salmonella Stanley, and an overview of respiratory virus epidemiology in the EU/EEA.

source: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
title: Hantavirus-associated cluster of illness on a cruise ship: ECDC assessment and recommendations
summary: As of 6 May 2026, seven cases have been reported in a hantavirus-associated cluster of illness on a cruise ship, including three deaths, one critically ill, two symptomatic and one with unknown status.

source: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
title: Cruise ship hantavirus outbreak: ECDC response activated
summary: ECDC has deployed an expert from the EU Health Task Force to the cruise ship affected by the Andes hantavirus outbreak, as part of a joint effort to investigate the outbreak and coordinate the public health response together with relevant Member States.

source: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
title: Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship under investigation: risk for Europe very low
summary: Hantavirus infection has been laboratory-confirmed in two individuals in connection with the cluster of illness reported on a cruise ship.

data_correction.reason: Cruise passenger/crew population numbers were ignored when they looked like non-case counts.
notes: Ignored suspected_cases >= 100 as likely passenger/crew count.
Ignored total_identified_cases >= 100 as likely passenger/crew count.

source: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
title: Suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship under investigation: risk for Europeans very low
summary: A cluster of severe acute respiratory illness, including three deaths and one severely ill individual, has been reported among passengers on a cruise ship travelling in the Atlantic and registered in the Netherlands. The ship is currently located off the coast of Cabo Verde, with 149 people on board.

source: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control
title: ECDC monitoring outbreak associated with cruise ship
summary: ECDC is aware of suspected cases of hantavirus infection, including three deaths, associated with a cruise ship travelling in the Atlantic.

source: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control

source: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention
title: Africa CDC disease outbreak report
summary: Statement on Multi-Country Hantavirus Cluster Associated with Cruise Ship Travel – Africa CDC Skip to content Email: communications@africacdc.org Search Search News Statement on Multi-Country Hantavirus Cluster Associated with Cruise Ship Travel Date: 5 May 2026 The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) is closely monitoring reports of a cluster of hantavirus infections identified among individuals travelling on the international cruise ship MV Hondius, which departed Ushuaia, Argentina, on 20 March 2026 for the Canary Islands via Cabo Verde. On 2 May 2026, a cluster of severe respiratory illness was reported among passengers aboard the vessel, which carried a total of 147 individuals, including crew. As of 4 May 2026, seven cases of hantavirus infection have been identified (two laboratory-confirmed and five suspected cases).
